mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, producing a positive direction over a 20-day horizon that boosts equity valuations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.2%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, producing a negative direction over a 60-day horizon that compresses equity multiples.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.2% and remains elevated for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, producing a negative direction over a 20-day horizon that leads to earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when both INDPRO and HOUST decline simultaneously compared to periods where only one declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances reflect resilient household spending that offsets tight financial conditions, producing a positive direction over a 60-day horizon that supports equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, contradicting the resilience premise.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins through higher volume and pricing power, producing a positive direction over a 20-day horizon that generates equity outperformance.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth rate against 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 240, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, producing a negative direction over a 5-day horizon that compresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL breaches 240 and stays above it for one month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with stagnant housing starts indicates a labor market that is absorbing slack without triggering construction-led credit expansion, producing a negative direction over a 5-day horizon that mutes earnings growth.
regime_hint: labor market decoupling from housing cycle
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly lower when PAYEMS rises and HOUST stagnates compared to periods of synchronized labor-housing growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, rising industrial production paired with stable inflation indicates improved capacity utilization without price pressures, producing a positive direction over a 60-day horizon that supports equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside stable CPIAUCSL, indicating that capacity gains are not translating to equity outperformance.
